perturbation.original_text: 1.21 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with the efforts and resources [***] of similar market potential, at a similar stage in development or product lifecycle, taking into account the stage of development or product lifecycle of other of [***] product candidates, safety and efficacy, product profile, cost of goods, the competitiveness of the marketplace, such company's patent position with respect to such product (including such company's ability to obtain or enforce, or have obtained or enforced, such patent rights), the Third Party patent landscape relevant to the product, the regulatory structure involved, the likelihood of regulatory approval, the likelihood and extent of anticipated or actual profitability of the applicable product, and other technical, legal, scientific and medical considerations. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) promptly assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s) who are held accountable for progress and monitor such progress on an on-going basis, (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources designed to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" or "Best Efforts" means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with the efforts and resources [***] of similar market potential, at a similar stage in development or product lifecycle, taking into account the stage of development or product lifecycle of other of [***] product candidates, safety and efficacy, product profile, cost of goods, the competitiveness of the marketplace, such company's patent position with respect to such product (including such company's ability to obtain or enforce, or have obtained or enforced, such patent rights), the Third Party patent landscape relevant to the product, the regulatory structure involved, the likelihood of regulatory approval, the likelihood and extent of anticipated or actual profitability of the applicable product, and other technical, legal, scientific and medical considerations. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) promptly assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s) who are held accountable for progress and monitor such progress on an on-going basis, (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources designed to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
perturbation.explanation: By defining Commercially Reasonable Efforts and Best Efforts as interchangable, the agreement now suffers from an in-text contradiction since these terms have different legal meanings. The insertion causes confusion on the level of effort required. Throughout the agreement, Commercially Reasonable Efforts is used, but the introduction of "Best Efforts" as interchangable makes the obligations less defined and enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Aimmune shall be responsible for the Development of the Product as set forth herein. Aimmune, itself or with or through its Affiliates and Sublicensees, shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to perform the Development Activities for the Product to (i) achieve the development milestones set forth in Section 7.2, and (ii) obtain Regulatory Approval for the Product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction because it equates "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" with "Best Efforts." The contradicted text requires Aimmune to use "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" to develop the Product and obtain Regulatory Approval. By defining these two terms as interchangeable, the agreement introduces ambiguity regarding the level of effort required, undermining the enforceability of Aimmune's obligation in the contradicted text since 'Best Efforts' implies a higher standard of diligence than 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts.'

perturbation.original_text: 1.64 "Regulatory Approval" means, with respect to any biopharmaceutical product in any regulatory jurisdiction for a given indication, approval from the applicable Regulatory Authority permitting the manufacture, sale, distribution or Commercialization of such biopharmaceutical product in such regulatory jurisdiction for such indication in accordance with Applicable Law, including any Pricing Approvals.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.64 "Regulatory Approval" or "Marketing Approval" means, with respect to any biopharmaceutical product in any regulatory jurisdiction for a given indication, approval from the applicable Regulatory Authority permitting the manufacture, sale, distribution or Commercialization of such biopharmaceutical product in such regulatory jurisdiction for such indication in accordance with Applicable Law, including any Pricing Approvals.
perturbation.explanation: The insertion creates uncertainty because in some jurisdictions, Regulatory Approval is broader than Marketing Approval and includes items beyond just permitting Commercialization (such as manufacturing). Other parts of the agreement will now be contradict by this since those terms are now defined as being the same. If someone wants to just get marketing approval and not regulatory, that may not be a correct approach. The modified definition of the term introduces contradiction and ambiguity, since they're related but are also very specific different aspects
perturbation.contradicted_text: Aimmune shall be responsible for the preparation of all Regulatory Materials necessary or desirable for obtaining and maintaining the Regulatory Approvals for the Product and Aimmune shall submit such Regulatory Materials, as applicable, to the applicable Governmental Authorities. For clarity, to the extent allowed by Applicable Law, all Regulatory Approvals for the Product shall be held and owned by Aimmune in its name.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Regulatory Approval' to include 'Marketing Approval' creates a strong contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text'. The change equates the two terms, while in reality, 'Regulatory Approval' can be broader. The contradicted text assigns Aimmune the responsibility for obtaining and maintaining 'Regulatory Approvals'. If 'Regulatory Approval' is now synonymous with 'Marketing Approval', Aimmune's responsibility is narrowed, potentially excluding other regulatory aspects beyond just marketing. This creates uncertainty and contradiction about the scope of Aimmune's obligations, making it unclear whether they are responsible for all aspects of regulatory approval or only marketing approval, contradicting the original intent and creating ambiguity and thus a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.22 "Control" or "Controlled by" means, with respect to any Know-How, Invention, Patent, technology, copyright, trademark or other intellectual property right, possession by a Party or its Affiliates (whether by ownership, license grant or other means) of the legal right to grant the right to access or use, or to grant a license or a sublicense to, such Know-How, Invention, Patent, technology, copyright, trademark or other intellectual property right as provided for herein without violating the proprietary rights of any Third Party or any terms of any agreement or other arrangement between such Party (or any of its Affiliates) and any Third Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.22 "Control" or "Ownership" means, with respect to any Know-How, Invention, Patent, technology, copyright, trademark or other intellectual property right, possession by a Party or its Affiliates (whether by ownership, license grant or other means) of the legal right to grant the right to access or use, or to grant a license or a sublicense to, such Know-How, Invention, Patent, technology, copyright, trademark or other intellectual property right as provided for herein without violating the proprietary rights of any Third Party or any terms of any agreement or other arrangement between such Party (or any of its Affiliates) and any Third Party.
perturbation.explanation: By defining Control and Ownership as interchangable, it ignores the fact that you can 'control' something without 'owning' it, such as through a license. The insertion causes confusion as to what a party truly has rights to and can grant those rights to something it controls (but does not own). If the party doesn't 'own' something, how can it grant rights? This change introduces ambiguity into the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Xencor hereby grants to Aimmune during the Term an exclusive, worldwide, payment-bearing license under and with respect to Xencor Patents and Xencor's interest in Joint Collaboration Patents, and a non-exclusive, payment bearing license under and with respect to Xencor Know-How, in each case, with the right to sublicense solely in accordance with Section 2.3.2, solely to Develop, Manufacture and Commercialize the Product in and for the Licensed Field; provided that notwithstanding the foregoing, Xencor shall retain the right under and with respect to Xencor Patents and Xencor's interest in Joint Collaboration Patents to the extent necessary to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text defines 'Control' and 'Ownership' interchangeably, while the original text correctly defined 'Control' as including rights obtained through licensing. The contradicted text grants a license to Aimmune under Xencor Patents. By conflating 'Control' and 'Ownership', the change introduces a contradiction because if Xencor only 'controls' the patents via a license (and doesn't own them), the changed definition casts doubt on Xencor's ability to grant sublicenses under the agreement, thus directly contradicting the license grant in the contradicted text. The change directly affects the rights and obligations related to licensing, creating a direct conflict within the document and impacting the enforceability of the licensing agreement.
